content: Curiosity-driven exploration by AI agents inherently self-regulates to avoid overloading working memory, because agents only encode novel, unpredicted stimuli into working memory for active reasoning instead of clogging capacity with redundant familiar information. This creates a naturally emergent bottleneck that improves rather than impairs reasoning, by aligning working memory load to an agent's fixed capacity limit.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
